items.question: Be careful with the knife — you might cut ___ .
explanations: 'Yourselves' is for more than one person.

items.question: The children really enjoyed ___ at the party.
explanations: 'Themself' isn't standard for a plural subject.

items.question: I don't need help — I'll do it ___ .
She taught ___ to play the guitar.
He lives ___ himself in the countryside. (alone)
Please help ___ to some cake. (to one guest)
explanations: 'Yourselves' is for more than one person.

items.question: The manager ___ came to apologise. (emphasis)
explanations: Correct! Emphasis after the noun: 'the manager herself'.
'Themselves' is plural and the wrong person.

items.question: Correct this: 'I feel myself much better today.'
options: I feel myself much better today.
I feel me much better today.
I am feeling myself better today.
explanations: Correct! 'Feel' doesn't take a reflexive: 'I feel much better'.
'Feel myself better' is a common error.
'Feeling myself better' still wrongly adds the reflexive.

items.question: Correct this: 'Did you paint the room by yourselves?' (one person)
options: Did you paint the room by yourself?
Did you paint the room by yourselves?
Did you paint the room by you?
Did you paint the room yourself by?
explanations: 'Yourselves' is for more than one person.

items.question: When do you use a reflexive pronoun?
options: When the subject and object are the same person.
explanations: Correct! Use it when the subject and object are the same (or for emphasis).
Many verbs (wash, feel) don't take a reflexive.
Reflexives are used in all sentence types, not just questions.
